reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21 — the custom GLiNER decoder was INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED FIRST in headless Chrome (a from-scratch reimplementation of the MIT gliner library, checked against known sentences: Barack Obama->person, Apple->company, 1976->date, USS Enterprise->spaceship all correct on both fp32 and q8), then the deployed page driven end-to-end (News + Sci-fi examples, arbitrary types). This unblocks a previously-BLOCKED catalogue entry (transformers.js has no GLiNER class) by running the ONNX directly. Text-only demo.

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY + CORRECTNESS verified in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web 1.21.0 WASM, q8) BEFORE building, against known sentences: 'Barack Obama was born in Honolulu Hawaii' [person,location] -> person: Barack Obama (0.98), location: Honolulu (0.82), Hawaii (0.62); 'Apple was founded by Steve Jobs in California in 1976' -> company/person/location/date all correct; 'Tesla ... Elon Musk apologized on Monday' -> company: Tesla, person: Elon Musk, day: Monday. fp32 and q8 agree; q8 ~58 ms. Deployed page (scripts/validate-gliner.mjs 10/10): the Sci-fi example extracts ARBITRARY types (spaceship: USS Enterprise, planet: Vulcan). The tags are the model's real scored spans, not canned.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a module Worker: a transformers.js AutoTokenizer + a raw onnxruntime-web session, with the GLiNER prompt/span preprocessing + decode reimplemented from the MIT gliner library.

rubric.evidence: Prompt '<<ENT>> type1 <<ENT>> type2 ... <<SEP>> word1 word2 ...' -> inputs input_ids/attention_mask/words_mask/text_lengths/span_idx/span_mask (spans up to max_width 12) -> logits[startWord, width, entityType] -> sigmoid -> greedy non-overlapping selection. A DISTINCT capability: OPEN-VOCABULARY NER (any types at runtime), vs the built NER/POS demos (fixed BIO classes) and vs zero-shot-classification (whole-text labels, not spans).
rubric.notes: The entity types are part of the INPUT, so changing them re-runs with a new schema and no retraining — the core property the demo exists to show.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (GLiNER small v2.1, deberta-v3-small span encoder, linked), task (zero-shot/open-vocab NER), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21 + a transformers.js tokenizer — noting transformers.js has no GLiNER pipeline), the prompt/span I/O, quantisation (q8), download (~183 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). No invented API.
rubric.notes: onnxruntime-web is pinned in THIS worker only; the preprocessing/decode credit the MIT gliner library (github urchade/GLiNER) in both the code and the UI.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'onnx-community/gliner_small-v2.1', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:183}). The ONNX is fetched THROUGH Cache Storage under a key carrying the model-id path, and the tokenizer files are cached by transformers.js under the same repo -> lib/model-cache.js scans '/onnx-community/gliner_small-v2.1' and auto-initialises on a returning visit with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download. Verified: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it flipped to ready and extracted the News example.
rubric.notes: 183 MB is a real download, so the never-silently-download guarantee + auto-init on return both matter; the per-model clear-cache control holds via the shared loader.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (absent) -> ready (auto-extracts the News example) -> debounced live re-extract on text OR entity-type edits + example chips; a busy/queued guard coalesces rapid edits; error path via the shared loader + a per-run try/catch surfaced in the status line. Verified download -> ready -> News -> Sci-fi end-to-end.
rubric.notes: Empty text or no types clears to plain text. Threshold is fixed at 0.5 (a slider is a possible follow-up).

rubric.evidence: An editable text area AND an editable comma-separated entity-type field (the star control — type any types), plus four cross-domain example chips (News, Medical: medication/dosage/frequency/condition, Shopping: product/brand/retailer/price, Sci-fi: character/spaceship/planet) that show non-standard types working.
rubric.notes: The examples are chosen so the zero-shot property is unmistakable — medication/dosage and spaceship/planet are not classes any fixed NER model ships with.

rubric.evidence: Every tag is the model's real scored span (score shown on hover), selected by the verified greedy decode; ambiguous inputs simply yield fewer/lower tags. No canned entities. Text-only demo, so no image/audio provenance concerns; the reimplemented decode was checked against the reference library's outputs before shipping.
rubric.notes: Honest about method: the UI + code state the preprocessing/decode reimplement the MIT gliner library and were verified against known sentences.

rubric.evidence: The output region is role=status aria-live=polite; the text area + types input are labelled; example chips are native buttons; each entity mark has a title (type + score) and a visible type sublabel, so tags aren't conveyed by colour alone.
rubric.notes: Colours are hashed per type for scannability, but the inline type sublabel carries the meaning for non-colour users.

rubric.evidence: Full-width fields (box-sizing: border-box), flex-wrap chips + legend, and highlighted output that wraps. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 360px (validate-gliner.mjs), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Text-only layout; verified desktop+mobile. Subpage ladder is a recorded follow-up.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; a q8 deberta-v3-small forward pass over a sentence is ~58 ms, and a 500 ms input debounce + busy/queued coalescing keep live editing smooth. The ~183 MB q8 download is a one-time cost.
rubric.notes: num_spans grows with text length x max_width; for paragraph-scale input the span tensor is larger but still sub-second — a length cap is a possible follow-up for very long documents.

guidanceConsulted.query: run a raw ONNX model + tokenizer off the main thread; keep a live text/types editor responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move inference off the main thread; keep interactions responsive (INP); debounce live input; coalesce rapid edits.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — extraction runs in a module Worker (transformers.js tokenizer + onnxruntime-web); edits are debounced 500 ms with a busy/queued guard.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: ~58 ms per sentence; page responsive while editing text + types

guidanceConsulted.query: full-width form controls + wrapping highlighted output that never overflows horizontally
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use box-sizing: border-box for full-width inputs; flex-wrap chips/legend; let highlighted text wrap.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — text area + types input are width:100% box-sizing:border-box; chips + legend flex-wrap; output wraps.

guidanceConsulted.query: announce a changing extraction result to assistive tech; label editable text + type controls; avoid colour-only tags
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce the live result via role=status aria-live; label inputs; give each highlight a text label, not just colour.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — output is role=status aria-live=polite; fields are labelled; each entity mark carries a type sublabel + title, not colour alone.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: status announces the entity + type count; tags show their type inline

openQuestions: Should the demo expose a confidence-threshold slider, given recall/precision trades off visibly as you lower it?
For long documents, is a per-request word cap (or chunking) worth adding to bound the span tensor size?
followUpGoals: Add a nested-NER mode (the library's non-flat decode) so overlapping entities of different types can both surface.
Add a confidence-threshold slider and a 'copy as JSON' export of the extracted entities.
Build a basics/practical/wild subpage ladder (single clean sentence; a real document with a custom schema; adversarial/ambiguous text where zero-shot NER genuinely struggles).
summary: Zero-shot / open-vocabulary NER (GLiNER small v2.1) — a distinct capability (extract entities of ANY types named at runtime), separate from the built fixed-class NER/POS and from whole-text zero-shot-classification. transformers.js has no GLiNER class, so the ONNX runs via a per-worker onnxruntime-web pin with the prompt/span decode reimplemented from the MIT gliner library and INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED against known sentences (fp32 == q8). ~183 MB q8, ~58 ms. Index demo complete + verified 10/10; this unblocks a previously-blocked catalogue entry. Nested-NER + threshold slider + subpage ladder are recorded follow-ups.
